back_translation: Now therefore, O Israel, listen to the statutes and the judgments that I am teaching you to do, so that you may live and enter and possess the land that the Lord, the God of your fathers, is giving you.
You shall not add to the word that I am commanding you, and you shall not subtract from it, so that you may keep the commandments of the Lord your God that I am commanding you.
Your eyes have seen what the Lord did at Baal-peor; for every man who walked after Baal of Peor, the Lord your God destroyed him from your midst.
But you who cling to the Lord your God are alive, all of you, today.
See, I have taught you statutes and judgments just as the Lord my God commanded me, to do so in the midst of the land where you are about to enter to possess it.

And you shall keep and do, for this is your wisdom and your understanding in the eyes of the peoples, who will hear all these statutes and say: Surely this great nation is a wise and understanding people.
For what great nation is there that has gods near to it, as the Lord our God is whenever we call to Him?
And what great nation is there that has righteous statutes and judgments like all this Torah that I am setting before you today?

Only watch yourself and keep your soul diligently, lest you forget the things that your eyes have seen, and lest they depart from your heart all the days of your life. And you shall make them known to your sons and to your sons' sons.

The day that you stood before the Lord your God at Horeb, when the Lord said to me: Gather the people to Me, and I will let them hear My words, so that they may learn to fear Me all the days that they live on the ground, and that they shall teach their sons.
And you came near and stood under the mountain, and the mountain was burning with fire to the heart of the heavens, darkness, cloud, and deep gloom.
And the Lord spoke to you from the midst of the fire. The sound of words you heard, but a form you did not see—only a voice.
And He declared to you His covenant, which He commanded you to do—the Ten Words. And He wrote them on two tablets of stone.
And me the Lord commanded at that time to teach you statutes and judgments, for you to do them in the land that you are crossing over to possess.
So watch yourselves very carefully, for you did not see any form on the day the Lord spoke to you at Horeb from the midst of the fire,
lest you act corruptly and make for yourselves a carved image, a form of any figure, a likeness of a male or a female,
a likeness of any animal that is on the earth, a likeness of any winged bird that flies in the heavens,
a likeness of any creeping thing on the ground, a likeness of any fish that is in the waters below the earth.

And lest you lift up your eyes to the heavens and see the sun and the moon and the stars, all the host of the heavens, and be drawn away and bow down to them and serve them—those that the Lord your God has apportioned to all the peoples under all the heavens.
But you the Lord has taken and brought you out from the iron furnace, from Egypt, to be to Him a people of inheritance, as it is this day.

And the Lord was angry with me on account of your words, and He swore that I would not cross the Jordan and that I would not enter the good land that the Lord your God is giving you as an inheritance.
For I will die in this land; I am not crossing the Jordan. But you will cross over and possess this good land.

Watch yourselves, lest you forget the covenant of the Lord your God that He cut with you, and you make for yourselves a carved image, a likeness of anything that the Lord your God commanded you not to make.
For the Lord your God is a consuming fire; He is a jealous God.

When you father sons and sons of sons, and you have grown old in the land, and you act corruptly and make a carved image, a form of any thing, and do what is evil in the eyes of the Lord your God to provoke Him to anger,

I call against you today the heavens and the earth as witnesses, that perish you will perish quickly from upon the land that you are crossing the Jordan to possess. You will not make long days upon it, for utterly destroyed you will be destroyed.
And the Lord will scatter you among the peoples, and you will be left few in number among the nations to which the Lord will drive you.
And there you will serve gods, the work of the hands of man, wood and stone, which cannot see, cannot hear, cannot eat, and cannot smell.
But you will seek from there the Lord your God, and you will find Him when you search for Him with all your heart and with all your soul.
In your distress, and when all these things have found you, in the latter days, you will return to the Lord your God and listen to His voice.
For a merciful God is the Lord your God; He will not abandon you and He will not destroy you, and He will not forget the covenant of your fathers that He swore to them.

For ask now of the former days that were before you, from the day that God created man on the earth, and from one end of the heavens to the other end of the heavens: Has there ever been such a great thing as this, or has its like ever been heard?
Has a people ever heard the voice of God speaking from the midst of the fire, as you have heard, and lived?

Or has any god tried to come and take for himself a nation from the midst of another nation, by trials, by signs and by wonders, and by war, and by a strong hand, and by an outstretched arm, and by great terrors, according to all that the Lord your God did for you in Egypt before your eyes?
You were shown so that you would know that the Lord—He is God; there is no other besides Him.
From the heavens He made you hear His voice to discipline you, and on the earth He showed you His great fire, and His words you heard from the midst of the fire.
And because He loved your fathers, and He chose their offspring after them, and He brought you out by His presence, by His great power, from Egypt,
to dispossess nations greater and mightier than you from before you, to bring you in, to give you their land as an inheritance, as it is this day.
Know therefore today and return to your heart that the Lord—He is God in the heavens above and on the earth below; there is no other.

And you shall keep His statutes and His commandments that I am commanding you today, so that it may go well with you and with your sons after you, and so that you may prolong days upon the ground that the Lord your God is giving you, all the days.
Then Moses set apart three cities beyond the Jordan, toward the rising of the sun,
for a manslayer to flee there, who kills his neighbor without knowledge—and he did not hate him from yesterday and the day before—and he flees to one of these cities and lives:
Bezer in the wilderness in the land of the plateau, for the Reubenites; and Ramoth in Gilead, for the Gadites; and Golan in Bashan, for the Manassites.
And this is the Torah that Moses set before the sons of Israel.
These are the testimonies and the statutes and the judgments that Moses spoke to the sons of Israel when they came out from Egypt,
beyond the Jordan in the valley opposite Beth-peor, in the land of Sihon king of the Amorites who dwelt in Heshbon, whom Moses and the sons of Israel struck down when they came out from Egypt.
And they possessed his land and the land of Og king of Bashan, the two kings of the Amorites who were beyond the Jordan toward the rising of the sun,
from Aroer that is on the edge of the Brook of Arnon, and as far as Mount Siyon, that is Hermon,
and all the Arabah beyond the Jordan to the east, and as far as the Sea of the Arabah, below the slopes of Pisgah.
